title: Enforce operating loop via observable hooks, not documentation
context: Bench testing showed skills-as-files were read in 0/20 sessions; when identical content was guaranteed-delivered instead, FAIL→PASS flipped on every test. Delivery, not content quality, was the bottleneck. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: Gate the Stop hook on unverified product edits; capture PostToolUse events as observable facts (which files edited, which verify commands ran); block only at firm/strict firmness with max 2 blocks per turn to prevent lockout.
consequences: Loop delivery is now enforced by gating behavior, not hoped for via documentation
Product edits must be followed by verify-shaped commands (tests, typecheck, build) or the turn is blocked
Graceful degradation: at most 2 blocks per turn prevents indefinite lockout; session resets budget on each new prompt
State is per-session scratch in tmpdir, never persisted to .hunch/ (kept disposable and decision-graph-clean)
Never-throw posture: any hook error silently degrades to no-op, never breaks the session
Only applies at firm/strict firmness; advisory mode injects the loop but doesn't block
alternatives_rejected: Improve skill content/documentation — already tried, 0/20 adoption rate showed content alone doesn't drive behavior
Pre-turn delivery checks — would require reading agent intent, not observable facts
Persist state in .hunch/ — would pollute decision graph with machinery state
Strict unlimited blocks — would trap agents with no escape hatch
Advisory-only gating — too soft to enforce the loop in practice
